nodes.intro: Yellow grass hisses in the dry wind, and a lean wolf pads onto the old duelist's ring, hackles high.
nodes.subtitle: the Scrub-Runner
nodes.victory: Bramblefang dips its head, then melts back into the scrub, ceding the grasslands to you.

nodes.intro: A lone cactus-thing bristles beside a dry well, needles rattling like it's been waiting all day for company.
nodes.subtitle: the Prickly
nodes.victory: Pincushion Pete gives a stiff little bow and points you down the dusty road with one long spine.

nodes.intro: A fat little burrow-critter pokes its nose from a hole in the roadbank, cards clutched in tiny paws.
nodes.subtitle: the Nibbler
nodes.victory: Twitchell drops its whole hand in fright and vanishes down its burrow, leaving the path clear.

nodes.intro: A long-eared, coppery beast bounds circles round a fence post, too restless to stand for a proper greeting.
nodes.subtitle: the Bounder
nodes.victory: Coppertail flattens its ears, gives one sulky hop, and bounds off across the scrub.

nodes.intro: A great tusked beast dozes across the trail, and only stirs its heavy head when your cards come out.
nodes.subtitle: the Stampede
nodes.victory: Old Rumble heaves aside with a groaning sigh, clearing the whole road for you to pass.

nodes.intro: A gnarled root pokes from the trailside dirt, humming a cracked little tune that sets your teeth on edge.
nodes.subtitle: the Rooted
nodes.victory: Screechling lets out one last defeated warble and burrows sulkily back into the Rustmarch soil.

nodes.clue: Rusty played a sealed "gift deck" — handed to him by a hooded official of the court.
nodes.intro: A tinker looks up from a half-built contraption. "Oh! Here to duel? I'm Rusty — sort of the doorman. Beat me and I'll point you up the road." He grins and shuffles, dropping half his cards.
nodes.subtitle: A friendly warm-up in the workshop.
nodes.victory: "Wow, you're really good!" Rusty beams and points up the road. "There's a gambler in the parlor ahead. Tell her I said hi — actually, don't."

nodes.intro: In a violet-lit dessert parlor off the docks, a trembling pudding slides a stack of chips across the felt and grins.
nodes.subtitle: the Wobbling Wager
nodes.victory: Flan settles with a satisfied jiggle, sweeping the neon-glinting pot into its quivering embrace.

nodes.intro: Behind a curtain of amber beads, a gilded queen holds court over a humming table of masked riverboat gamblers.
nodes.subtitle: the House Always Stings
nodes.victory: Queen Bee reclines on her honeyed throne, the pot swept neatly into her waiting court.

nodes.intro: Belowdecks in a smoke-choked cargo hold, a green-slick rat shuffles a suspiciously sticky deck by lantern light.
nodes.subtitle: the Bilge-Deck Cheat
nodes.victory: Toxirat scurries off with the winnings clutched to his chest, cackling into the bilge-dark.

nodes.intro: On the misted upper deck, a pale feathered spirit floats above the table, murmuring to the swirling delta fog.
nodes.subtitle: the Fogbank Fortune
nodes.victory: Zemzelett dissolves half into mist, the pot vanishing with her into the swirling violet haze.

nodes.intro: In a buzzing back room strung with humming neon, a jittery wire-creature snaps the deck and deals before you sit.
nodes.subtitle: the Live-Wire Sharp
nodes.victory: Capparwire crackles with triumph, raking the pot in with a shower of tiny violet sparks.

nodes.intro: A sharp-dressed bird alights at the felt just long enough to fan a deck between manicured wingtips and wink.
nodes.subtitle: the Quick-Deal Dandy
nodes.victory: Fleetwing tips his little hat, pockets the pot, and flits out the window before the cards settle.

nodes.clue: A half-burnt ledger in the parlor names a hidden patron bankrolling the rigged games.
nodes.intro: Rusty's directions lead to a smoky parlor. A gambler fans her cards without looking up. "Rusty sent you? Cute. I'll wager you don't make it past the knight at the bridge."
nodes.subtitle: The gambler deals you in.
nodes.victory: Mireille flicks you a chip. "Beginner's luck. The knight at the bridge won't be half so charmed. Off you go, lucky."

nodes.intro: The broken gate of the Ashen Keep is chained shut by a brute the size of a siege ram.
nodes.subtitle: Keep-Breaker
nodes.victory: The gate-brute topples, and the ruined threshold lies open before you.

nodes.intro: A powder-scarred corporal stacks live charges along the Keep's inner rampart.
nodes.subtitle: Powder-Hand
nodes.victory: The grenadier's charges lie spent and cold along the silent rampart.

nodes.intro: A locked shieldwall of riot-troopers bars the scorched corridor beneath a sergeant's cold command.
nodes.subtitle: Shieldwall
nodes.victory: The shieldwall buckles and scatters, the corridor open behind them.

nodes.intro: A rigid fortress warden checks credentials at the sealed door to the Empress's ruined hall.
nodes.subtitle: Keymaster
nodes.victory: The warden's seals fail, and the sealed door swings inward at last.

nodes.intro: A flametrooper scours hexed cards from the Keep's halls, leaving blackened stone in his wake.
nodes.subtitle: Ash-Maker
nodes.victory: The pyre-sergeant's flame dies, and the smoke clears from the scorched hall.

nodes.intro: Something Wither-warped skitters through the Keep's flooded cellars, dragging hexed cards behind it.
nodes.subtitle: the Wither-Touched
nodes.victory: The crawler unravels into ash and stagnant water, the cellar quiet again.

nodes.clue: Aldous swears the Empress died at the table — with a hexed card already in play.
nodes.intro: A bridge, and a knight in weathered armor. "The gambler's coin says you'll fall here. Prove her right, or prove her wrong — but you do not cross without a duel."
nodes.subtitle: The knight bars the bridge.
nodes.victory: The knight lowers his shield. "The crossing is yours. Beyond lies the witch — guard your cards, and your wits."

nodes.intro: The bog exhales a green miasma, and something vast and gurgling unfurls a hundred grinning mouths from the muck.
nodes.subtitle: Rot-Breather of the Sunken Court
nodes.victory: The Fenmaw collapses into a slick of steaming rot, its many mouths falling silent one wet gasp at a time.

nodes.intro: Threads of wet violet light drift between the reeds, and a robed thing turns four writhing tendrils toward your skull.
nodes.subtitle: the Palate of Thought
nodes.victory: The Palate's tendrils curl inward and shrivel, its stolen thoughts spilling back into the fen like drowned whispers.

nodes.intro: A single vast eye rises from the black water, its slit pupil narrowing as it appraises the worth of your blood.
nodes.subtitle: Warden of Blighted Ledgers
nodes.victory: The great eye clouds, weeps a thin magenta ichor, and sinks beneath the ledger-scummed water forever.

nodes.intro: A wail rolls across the marsh, curdling the water and bending the reeds flat, as a mouth of pure sound tears open in the mist.
nodes.subtitle: the Dirge That Walks
nodes.victory: The scream chokes into a whistle, then nothing, and the Hexfen rings with a silence that had been missing for a hundred years.

nodes.intro: Sickly blue-magenta flame skates across the swamp water, coiling upward into a serpent whose every hiss spits burning hex.
nodes.subtitle: the Bogfire Serpent
nodes.victory: The bogfire serpent gutters, drowns in its own reflection, and the marsh goes cold and dark where it died.

nodes.intro: Jagged magenta crystal juts from the mire, and within its heart an armored shape unfolds glittering claws that ring like cracked bells.
nodes.subtitle: the Hexfen Reliquary
nodes.victory: Quartzmaw's crystalline hide shatters into a thousand dimming shards, each one hissing as the fen's corruption bleeds out of it.

nodes.clue: Vesper's hex-craft orders bear a wax seal: the mark of the Lord Regent.
nodes.intro: Beyond the bridge, a witch's parlor glitters with hexes. "The knight let you pass? How careless of him. I serve the Grandmaster, and I do so love to make examples."
nodes.subtitle: The hexweaver's glittering parlor.
nodes.victory: Her hexes unravel in a puff of smoke. "Fine! Go on to him, then. Koll has never lost. You'll make a lovely cautionary tale."

nodes.intro: At the gilded outer gate, where the capital's gold runs molten red, a crimson dragon uncoils across your path.
nodes.subtitle: the Ember-Marshal
nodes.victory: The Ember-Marshal collapses to cooling slag, and the first gate of the Obsidian Court groans open.

nodes.intro: Beneath vaulted obsidian arches carved with a thousand dead claimants, the eldest dragon of Auveraine awaits its rite.
nodes.subtitle: Keeper of the Ancient Bloodright
nodes.victory: Sathyrion lowers its ancient head at last, conceding the rite it has guarded since the first throne.

nodes.intro: The corridor itself rears up as a wyrm of black volcanic glass, your fractured reflection sneering back a thousandfold.
nodes.subtitle: the Black Glass Warden
nodes.victory: Nyxhalor shatters into a rain of obsidian shards, clearing the mirrored hall to the inner Court.

nodes.intro: In the tilting-yard of blood before the inner keep, an infernal knight lowers a lance wreathed in courtly flame.
nodes.subtitle: First Lance of the Infernal Guard
nodes.victory: Ser Malecaste topples from his smoldering steed, and the Infernal Guard parts before you.

nodes.intro: A second faceless horseman wheels from the shadow of the vacant throne, hooves striking sparks from black gold.
nodes.subtitle: Herald of the Empty Throne
nodes.victory: The Second Rider dissolves into cold smoke, and the way to Corvane's empty seat lies open.

nodes.intro: Before the dais of the empty throne, a colossal engine of fused riders and rotating blades roars to life.
nodes.subtitle: Corvane's Last Warden
nodes.victory: The Deathwheel seizes and collapses into scrap and ash, leaving only the empty throne ahead.

nodes.clue: Koll was never the master. The Regent — Corvane — steps from behind the throne he kept empty.
nodes.intro: At last, the throne room. The Grandmaster does not rise. "Vesper's hexes failed. Curious. Sit. Let us see if you are the one the prophecies kept boring me with."
nodes.subtitle: The grandmaster's throne. None have won.
nodes.victory: Koll is silent a long moment, then inclines his head. "The streak ends. The table is yours, champion — and the kingdom will learn your name."

nodes.clue: The Bloodright is broken and remade — Auveraine has its true heir.
nodes.intro: Koll steps aside, and the kindly Regent who guided you from the start folds his hands and smiles — the villain, at last, in plain sight.
nodes.subtitle: the Provident
nodes.victory: Corvane crumbles as the hexed cards fall dead from his hand, and the throne of Auveraine stands open for its true heir.

nodes.intro: Where the mountains bleed molten gold, the Cinder Sovereign Ifrit rules a throne no mortal crown could survive.
nodes.subtitle: the Cinder Sovereign
nodes.victory: The caldera cools to glass, and Ifrit bows his burning head to a fire greater than his own.

nodes.intro: Above the cloud-veiled peaks glides Quetzalcoatl, the Feathered Dawn, who tests all who climb toward the morning.
nodes.subtitle: the Feathered Dawn
nodes.victory: The great serpent spirals once into the sunrise and is gone, leaving a single luminous feather at your feet.

nodes.intro: Where the thunderheads never break, the ravenous thunderbird Zu shrieks its dominion over a stolen sky.
nodes.subtitle: the Storm That Steals the Sky
nodes.victory: Zu's final screech scatters into distant thunder, and the long-caged sky opens blue above you.

nodes.intro: Down the endless torchlit corridor shuffles the Tonberry King, lantern in hand, closing the distance no man has outrun.
nodes.subtitle: He Who Walks Without Haste
nodes.victory: The lantern gutters and dims, the knife lowers, and for the first time in ages the little king simply stops.

nodes.intro: Deep in the strangling green sleeps the Emerald Witch, whose garden is grown from the bones of every crown before.
nodes.subtitle: Keeper of the Verdant Reckoning
nodes.victory: The thorns wither and part, and the Emerald Witch dissolves into falling petals, her garden yours to walk.

nodes.intro: Beneath the deepest vault of Auveraine waits the Shadowblood Queen, from whose ancient duel all the Bloodright was born.
nodes.subtitle: the Throne Beneath the Throne
nodes.victory: The undying queen closes her eyes at last, her shadow-throne crumbling to light as the true crown passes to you.

opponents.intro: Oh, a challenger! Go easy on me, I'm still learning the ropes.
opponents.name: Rusty
opponents.onLose: Aw, beans. Good game though — you've got a real knack for this!
opponents.onWin: I won? I WON! Wait 'til the workshop hears about this!
opponents.persona: a cheerful, clumsy novice tinkerer who plays it safe and roots for everyone
opponents.taunt: Huh, that actually worked? Beginner's luck, I swear!
opponents.tauntAhead: Wait, am I actually ahead? Don't tell anyone!
opponents.tauntBehind: Okay, okay, I can still turn this around… probably.
opponents.title: the Tinker

opponents.intro: Care to make it interesting? I do love long odds.
opponents.name: Mireille
opponents.onLose: Well played. I'll want a rematch — double or nothing.
opponents.onWin: The house always wins. Better luck next hand.
opponents.persona: a smooth, confident card sharp who loves a risky, opportunistic play
opponents.taunt: Read 'em and weep, darling.
opponents.tauntAhead: The cards are falling my way — as they always do.
opponents.tauntBehind: A losing hand is just a setup for a bigger bluff.
opponents.title: the Gambler

opponents.intro: Stand and be tested. Hold your ground, and we shall see who endures.
opponents.name: Sir Aldous
opponents.onLose: Bested, fair and square. Well met, champion.
opponents.onWin: A hard-fought field. You have my respect.
opponents.persona: a stoic, honorable knight who fights a patient war of territory and walls
opponents.taunt: A line held is a battle half won.
opponents.tauntAhead: The field is mine. Press, and you will break upon it.
opponents.tauntBehind: A knight does not yield. I will hold to the last.
opponents.title: the Steadfast

opponents.intro: Let's see how your little cards fare against my hexes, hm?
opponents.name: Vesper
opponents.onLose: Impossible… my hexes, undone? You'll regret this.
opponents.onWin: Did you really think you'd unravel me?
opponents.persona: a sly, theatrical witch who delights in buffs, hexes and aggressive swings
opponents.taunt: Wither. Crumble. How delicious.
opponents.tauntAhead: You're already unraveling. I do love to watch.
opponents.tauntBehind: No matter — a hex always has the last laugh.
opponents.title: the Hexweaver

opponents.intro: Many sit at this table. None rise victorious. Begin.
opponents.name: Grandmaster Koll
opponents.onLose: …So. The streak ends. You have earned your place.
opponents.onWin: As expected. The throne remains mine.
opponents.persona: a calm, ruthless grandmaster who has not lost a duel in living memory
opponents.taunt: Every move you make, I made three turns ago.
opponents.tauntAhead: It is already decided. You simply have not realized.
opponents.tauntBehind: Curious. I will allow you this small moment.
opponents.title: the Unbeaten

opponents.intro: You smell like far-off cities, little traveler. Bramblefang runs these grasses. Show teeth, or run.
opponents.name: Bramblefang
opponents.onLose: You... run faster than the pack. Go. The grass is yours.
opponents.onWin: The Rustmarch keeps its runners. Go home, soft one.
opponents.persona: A pack-minded aggressor who floods the board early and hunts weak cards down.
opponents.taunt: Pack circles slow. Then pack does not.
opponents.tauntAhead: You bleed already. I taste the win on the wind.
opponents.tauntBehind: Grr... one wolf falls. The grass still hides more.
opponents.title: the Scrub-Runner

opponents.intro: Howdy. Don't hug me. Folks always try. Now sit, and let's prick this out proper.
opponents.name: Pincushion Pete
opponents.onLose: Well, shoot. Ya out-pricked a cactus. Take yer road, sharp one.
opponents.onWin: Told ya not to hug me. Off ya go, all poked full.
opponents.persona: A patient defensive stallwaller who chips away with steady, needling damage.
opponents.taunt: One needle. Then another. I got a thousand more, friend.
opponents.tauntAhead: Ha! Full of holes already? Stand still, I ain't done.
opponents.tauntBehind: Yeouch. Losin' my points quick as I lose needles.
opponents.title: the Prickly

opponents.intro: Oh! Oh, a duel? You want duel? Twitchell duels! Don't step on me. Please.
opponents.name: Twitchell
opponents.onLose: Squeeeak! Too big, too big! Twitchell go now. Bye bye!
opponents.onWin: Twitchell win! Twitchell win! Now Twitchell hide before you change mind!
opponents.persona: A skittish swarm-player who plays many tiny cards and scurries out of danger.
opponents.taunt: Little, little, little cards. Lots of little. Squeak!
opponents.tauntAhead: Nibble nibble! Winning! Twitchell winning, hee!
opponents.tauntBehind: Eep! Losing losing! Where's my hole, where's my hole?!
opponents.title: the Nibbler

opponents.intro: Quick quick! You slow, I fast. Coppertail no wait for slow duelists. Go!
opponents.name: Coppertail
opponents.onLose: You... faster than a Coppertail? Impossible. Fine. This road yours.
opponents.onWin: Ha! Coppertail bound over you like a low fence. Bye, slow-legs!
opponents.persona: A fast, hit-and-run tempo player who leaps ahead early and never sits still.
opponents.taunt: Hop here. Hop there. You never catch Coppertail.
opponents.tauntAhead: Too far ahead! Bound, bound, bound! You still back there?
opponents.tauntBehind: Ears down. Coppertail hop wrong way. Bad, bad hops.
opponents.title: the Bounder

opponents.intro: Mmm. A little one comes to duel Old Rumble. Take your time. I have plenty.
opponents.name: Old Rumble
opponents.onLose: You struck before Rumble could stand tall. Well done, small one. Pass.
opponents.onWin: The ground shakes, little one goes home. Watch your step next time.
opponents.persona: A slow, heavy ramp-player who builds up huge cards and crashes through late.
opponents.taunt: Slow feet. Heavy feet. Feet that do not stop.
opponents.tauntAhead: Rumble builds. Rumble grows. Soon Rumble rolls right over you.
opponents.tauntBehind: Hrmph. Small and fast bites the big and slow. Not fair.
opponents.title: the Stampede

opponents.intro: ...you dug me up for a DUEL? Fine. Cover your ears, sprout. I sing off-key.
opponents.name: Screechling
opponents.onLose: Fine. FINE. Pull me up and drag me along, victor. I'll scream the whole way.
opponents.onWin: AAAIEEE! ...ahem. That's my winning note. Now bury me back, loud-loser.
opponents.persona: A disruptive control-player who locks the board down and wails to throw you off.
opponents.taunt: Roots under your feet. You not going anywhere.
opponents.tauntAhead: SCREEEE— oh, sorry. That one meant you're losing.
opponents.tauntBehind: Wilting... wilting... don't you dare replant me smug.
opponents.title: the Rooted

opponents.intro: Easy now, don't jostle me. I wobble, I wager, but darlin', I never spill a winning hand.
opponents.name: Flan
opponents.onLose: Cracked at last... scrape me up and pour me a drink.
opponents.onWin: Wobble on home, friend. The pot's mine, all sweet and set.
opponents.persona: A quivering, sugar-slick swamp pudding of a hustler who trembles with every bet but never folds.
opponents.taunt: Steady, steady... watch me shake you down to nothin'.
opponents.tauntAhead: See how I quiver? That's the sweet flavor of your defeat, sugar.
opponents.tauntBehind: Knock me all you like, I jiggle right back up onto my feet.
opponents.title: the Wobbling Wager

opponents.intro: Welcome to my hive, sugar. The honey's sweet, but the house always keeps its sting.
opponents.name: Queen Bee
opponents.onLose: Stripped of my crown by a common drone? Unthinkable.
opponents.onWin: The hive feasts tonight. Thank you for the honey, dear.
opponents.persona: A regal, honey-tongued matron of the gambling hive who runs the table like a throne and taxes every loss.
opponents.taunt: Buzz all you want, darling. My workers already counted your coin.
opponents.tauntAhead: The comb is mine, and so is your purse. Do keep buzzing.
opponents.tauntBehind: A sting or two won't dethrone the queen, little drone.
opponents.title: the House Always Stings

opponents.intro: Pull up a crate, friend. Down here in the bilge, the cards are as crooked as I am.
opponents.name: Toxirat
opponents.onLose: Bilge take it! Cheated by an honest hand for once.
opponents.onWin: Squeak squeak, the pot's mine. Don't check my sleeves, now.
opponents.persona: A scheming, plague-slick riverboat rat who deals from the bottom of the deck and coughs green when he lies.
opponents.taunt: Heh. Sniff that? That's the smell of your luck rottin' away.
opponents.tauntAhead: I'm gnawin' through your deck like cheese, and it tastes fine.
opponents.tauntBehind: A cornered rat bites hardest, chum. Watch them fingers.
opponents.title: the Bilge-Deck Cheat

opponents.intro: The mist parts for me, wanderer. It whispered your hand before you drew it.
opponents.name: Zemzelett
opponents.onLose: The fog lied? No... I misread the omen. How rare.
opponents.onWin: The mist keeps its promises. Fortune drifts to me, as foretold.
opponents.persona: A dreamy, wind-borne fowl-spirit who reads fates in the delta mist and bets on omens no one else can see.
opponents.taunt: The fog already told me. You just haven't caught up, dear.
opponents.tauntAhead: I foresaw this drift. The current carries the pot to me.
opponents.tauntBehind: An omen clouds, but the wind still turns my way, love.
opponents.title: the Fogbank Fortune

opponents.intro: Ante up, ante up, let's go! I don't wait, sparky, and neither should you.
opponents.name: Capparwire
opponents.onLose: Blew a fuse... short-circuited at the wire. Rewire me.
opponents.onWin: Ha! Zap. Told ya I was quick. Pot's mine, spark's out.
opponents.persona: A crackling, high-strung tangle of nerves and copper who plays fast, talks faster, and shocks the slow.
opponents.taunt: Too slow, too slow! I already played three moves in my head.
opponents.tauntAhead: Feel that current? That's me, live and lightin' you up.
opponents.tauntBehind: Just a hiccup in the wire, friend. I bounce back hot.
opponents.title: the Live-Wire Sharp

opponents.intro: Evenin', friend. I deal quick and I fly quicker, so let's make this fun.
opponents.name: Fleetwing
opponents.onLose: Clipped my wings, did ya? Fine hand. I'll flutter off then.
opponents.onWin: And that's my cue to fly. Pleasure fleecin' you, friend.
opponents.persona: A dapper, fast-fluttering delta bird who flits table to table, all charm and speed, gone before the mark blinks.
opponents.taunt: Blink and you'll miss it, darling. I already have.
opponents.tauntAhead: Light as a feather, ahead by a mile. Try to keep pace.
opponents.tauntBehind: Ruffled, not grounded. This bird's got another gust in him.
opponents.title: the Quick-Deal Dandy

opponents.intro: Big walls fall. Small men fall faster. You come to the Empress's grave to play cards? Brakka will make it your grave too.
opponents.name: Brakka
opponents.onLose: Brakka... falls. Cold floor. Same floor She died on. Fitting.
opponents.onWin: Down you go. The Keep keeps another one. Brakka stacks the bones neat.
opponents.persona: A hulking garrison brute who guards the Ashen Keep's shattered gate; slow of speech, fond of crushing things.
opponents.taunt: Brakka smash. You bleed. Simple work.
opponents.tauntAhead: You crack like the old stones. Brakka pushes harder.
opponents.tauntBehind: Ha! Little sting. Brakka has been hit by walls.
opponents.title: Keep-Breaker

opponents.intro: Deck's loaded, fuse is short. This is a garrison, not a game, and you're standing in the kill zone. Let's see how long you last, recruit.
opponents.name: Corporal Vantle
opponents.onLose: Charge dud... and the blast came back on me. Noted. Filed. Damn.
opponents.onWin: Cleared. Sweep the ash, log the kill. Next in the line.
opponents.persona: A grim, precise siege soldier who reduces problems to blast radius and fuse length; speaks in clipped ordnance terms.
opponents.taunt: Pull the pin. Count down. Boom.
opponents.tauntAhead: Ranged and ranged. You're already inside the crater.
opponents.tauntBehind: Misfire. I've got more shells than you've got moves.
opponents.title: Powder-Hand

opponents.intro: The line stops here. Under me. Everyone thinks they're the one who gets through. The ash out front says otherwise. Draw and try.
opponents.name: Sergeant Halveck
opponents.onLose: The wall... breaks. Twenty years holding, and it breaks today. Pass, then.
opponents.onWin: Line holds. Another body for the pile. The Keep stays shut.
opponents.persona: A stone-faced crowd-breaker who holds the line and expects the world to break against him; disciplined, immovable.
opponents.taunt: Shield up. You don't pass.
opponents.tauntAhead: You push, I hold, you break. That's the order of it.
opponents.tauntBehind: The wall bends. The wall does not fall. Come again.
opponents.title: Shieldwall

opponents.intro: Halt. No clearance, no passage. This ground is sealed by order of the succession. If you want the seal broken, you'll break it across the table.
opponents.name: Warden Osk
opponents.onLose: Clearance... overridden. My seals mean nothing now. The Keep is yours.
opponents.onWin: Detained. Sealed. Struck from the roll. Move along, what's left of you.
opponents.persona: A cold, procedural fortress warden obsessed with clearance, protocol, and who is and isn't authorized to breathe here.
opponents.taunt: Access denied. Try the next card.
opponents.tauntAhead: You're flagged, logged, and losing. Protocol works.
opponents.tauntBehind: Irregular. I'll amend the record once I've corrected you.
opponents.title: Keymaster

opponents.intro: The Wither's in these stones, in the cards, in you. I burn what's hexed. Sit. Let's find out how much of you needs cleansing.
opponents.name: Pyre-Sergeant Vell
opponents.onLose: The flame... gutters out. Then the rot takes me too. Burn me after.
opponents.onWin: Purified. One more ash pile the Wither can't touch. Clean work.
opponents.persona: A zealous purifier tasked with burning the Wither out of the Keep; believes fire is the only clean thing left in Auveraine.
opponents.taunt: Fire finds everything. Even you.
opponents.tauntAhead: You're already smoke. You just haven't caught yet.
opponents.tauntBehind: Embers still bite. I'll relight and finish clean.
opponents.title: Ash-Maker

opponents.intro: Fresh one, warm one, down in the dark with us... The Keep drinks blood, and I do the pouring. Play. Let it in.
opponents.name: Nettle
opponents.onLose: Unraveling... back to rot, back to nothing. It'll... find you... later.
opponents.onWin: Down into the dark with the others. The Wither says thank you.
opponents.persona: A blight-warped wretch that skitters through the Keep's ducts and cellars; half-mad, whispering, more Wither than soldier.
opponents.taunt: Closer, closer. It wants you.
opponents.tauntAhead: The rot's in your deck now. Feel it spread?
opponents.tauntBehind: Hurts, yes, but crawlers heal wrong. We come back.
opponents.title: the Wither-Touched

opponents.intro: Come closer, little heir. Breathe deep the Hexfen air. Every card in my hand already reeks of the grave you'll share.
opponents.name: Malbrous the Fenmaw
opponents.onLose: Cut me down... my spores are already... in your lungs...
opponents.onWin: There now. Still. Sweet. You'll make such fragrant compost for the Wither.
opponents.persona: A bloated, many-mawed horror that speaks in overlapping wet voices, delighting in decay and the sweet stink of ruined cards.
opponents.taunt: Your deck festers. Can you not smell it curdling in your grip?
opponents.tauntAhead: Ripe, so ripe — I'll swallow your lead whole and belch out your defeat.
opponents.tauntBehind: Let me rot a while longer. Decay always wins the waiting game.
opponents.title: Rot-Breather of the Sunken Court

opponents.intro: I have already tasted the shape of your fear. Sit. Let me sample the rest, one thought at a time.
opponents.name: Vess'tharoth
opponents.onLose: Impossible — your mind was... noise... I could not read the... silence...
opponents.onWin: Delicious. I'll keep your final thought — it was the only interesting one.
opponents.persona: A cold, cerebral parasite that savors minds like delicacies and narrates your own doubts back to you.
opponents.taunt: Your next play — yes, that one. How dull your mind tastes.
opponents.tauntAhead: You lead, yet inside you already know how this ends. I can hear you knowing it.
opponents.tauntBehind: Every clever move you plan, I have eaten before you thought it.
opponents.title: the Palate of Thought

opponents.intro: State your claim to the Bloodright. I will find the flaw. I always find the flaw.
opponents.name: the Assessor
opponents.onLose: Recount — this cannot — the ledger does not permit such a... result...
opponents.onWin: Assessment complete. Claim denied. The succession has no place for the worthy.
opponents.persona: A floating, unblinking eye of the rigged succession, obsessed with judging, tallying, and finding every heir 'unfit.'
opponents.taunt: Noted. Catalogued. Your error joins a very long ledger.
opponents.tauntAhead: A lead? A clerical accident. The audit is not finished with you.
opponents.tauntBehind: Every card you spend, I record against you. The verdict is nearly written.
opponents.title: Warden of Blighted Ledgers

opponents.intro: HHHhhhear me — the fen has no silence left, and neither will YOU.
opponents.name: Hollowthroat
opponents.onLose: my voice — cracking — the dirge — going — quie—
opponents.onWin: Now you understand the note. Now you'll HUM it forever.
opponents.persona: A ragged, keening spirit whose every utterance is a shriek that frays the nerves and warps the very air of the duel.
opponents.taunt: SCREEEE — did that rattle your hand, little heir? Shall I do it again?
opponents.tauntAhead: You're winning? I'll scream until winning MEANS nothing.
opponents.tauntBehind: Behind? Good. Despair has the sharpest, sweetest pitch.
opponents.title: the Dirge That Walks

opponents.intro: Ssso. Fresh kindling wandersss into my fen. Sit — let me warm your ruin.
opponents.name: Cindrivax
opponents.onLose: my flame — drowning — the bog — swallowsss — its own... fire...
opponents.onWin: Ash. Ash and ssmoke. That's all a pretender's claim was ever worth.
opponents.persona: A serpentine horror of hexed marsh-gas flame, hissing and mocking, burning cards to ash with a glance.
opponents.taunt: Your cardsss curl so prettily when they burn. Play another.
opponents.tauntAhead: Ahead, are you? Ledsss burn brightest just before the ash.
opponents.tauntBehind: Sssmell that? That's your hope, smoldering. I've barely begun.
opponents.title: the Bogfire Serpent

opponents.intro: Skitter, skitter. Another soft thing to set in glass. Hold still — the crystal is patient.
opponents.name: Quartzmaw
opponents.onLose: my shell — fracturing — the hex-glass — will not — hold...
opponents.onWin: There. Perfectly preserved. You'll gleam in my reliquary for ages.
opponents.persona: An armored crustacean grown over with corrupted crystal, slow and patient, entombing every card and heir in creeping stone.
opponents.taunt: Click. Click. Your defenses crack like thin ice underfoot.
opponents.tauntAhead: Grow your lead. The bigger the prize, the finer the crystal I'll cage it in.
opponents.tauntBehind: Slow, yes. But stone always catches up. Feel it climbing your wrists?
opponents.title: the Hexfen Reliquary

opponents.intro: So the throne sends me carrion in a coat of arms. Kneel or burn, pretender. The gate to Corvane is paved with braver ash than yours.
opponents.name: Vermilion Ossuar
opponents.onLose: Impossible. My fire... you fed it your own and burned brighter. Pass, then.
opponents.onWin: There. Another crown-chaser rendered into embers on the Court's cold gold.
opponents.persona: A crimson wyrm bound in a warlord's harness, guarding the outer gates of the Obsidian Court; hot-blooded, brutal, contemptuous of soft petitioners.
opponents.taunt: Every card you lay smells of smoke already. Play faster, meat.
opponents.tauntAhead: You claw at my heels? Cute. I have not yet exhaled.
opponents.tauntBehind: A whelp with a lucky draw. The fire remembers your name now.
opponents.title: the Ember-Marshal

opponents.intro: I dueled kings before your bloodline learned to bleed. Show me you are worthy of the rite, or be forgotten with the rest.
opponents.name: Sathyrion the Elder
opponents.onLose: You have unwound an age of certainty. Go, then, and face what waits beyond me.
opponents.onWin: As it was ordained. The Bloodright does not err, and it did not choose you.
opponents.persona: An ancient archdragon who remembers the first Bloodright; cold, patrician, treats the duel as sacred rite and the challenger as a vulgar upstart.
opponents.taunt: Centuries of play, and you offer me... this? How quaint.
opponents.tauntAhead: A momentary lead. History is measured in ages, not in turns.
opponents.tauntBehind: Curious. The upstart has teeth. I shall have to remember them.
opponents.title: Keeper of the Ancient Bloodright

opponents.intro: I am the walls, the floor, the black mirror of this Court. Look into me, claimant. See how small the throne makes you.
opponents.name: Nyxhalor
opponents.onLose: You broke your own doubt against me and did not cut. Rare. Pass into the dark.
opponents.onWin: Your reflection dims. The black glass keeps it now, with all the others.
opponents.persona: A serpentine wyrm of living volcanic glass, part of the Court's very architecture; silent-menacing, speaks in cutting whispers, reflects the challenger's doubts.
opponents.taunt: Every crack in your strategy, I already wear as a facet.
opponents.tauntAhead: You advance across glass. Glass cuts the feet of the bold.
opponents.tauntBehind: Shatter me if you can. I only reflect what you fear to lose.
opponents.title: the Black Glass Warden

opponents.intro: Welcome, gentle claimant, to the Regent's hospitality. I will unhorse you with all the courtesy your death deserves.
opponents.name: Ser Malecaste
opponents.onLose: You kept your seat where princes fell. The First Lance salutes you. Ride on.
opponents.onWin: Unhorsed and unmade. I shall tell the Regent you died politely.
opponents.persona: A fallen knight astride a hell-forged steed, sworn to Corvane; courtly-cruel, mockingly chivalrous, treats slaughter as etiquette.
opponents.taunt: A poor showing. My steed grows bored, and a bored devil bites.
opponents.tauntAhead: Yield gracefully. There is dignity in it, and none in what I do to the stubborn.
opponents.tauntBehind: Well ridden, claimant. Let us see if your seat holds under fire.
opponents.title: First Lance of the Infernal Guard

opponents.intro: One rider passed before me; more will follow. I am the second omen, and I ride ahead of your ruin. The throne is empty for a reason, claimant.
opponents.name: the Second Rider
opponents.onLose: You outran the omen. Then ride on, claimant, and learn why the throne stands empty.
opponents.onWin: The omen is fulfilled. You reached the empty throne only to fall before it.
opponents.persona: A faceless second horseman of Corvane's guard, an omen more than a knight; ominous, prophetic, speaks of the empty throne and the coming reckoning.
opponents.taunt: Hear the hooves? That is the sound of your ending, drawing near.
opponents.tauntAhead: You outpace one rider. There are always more behind me.
opponents.tauntBehind: The herald does not fear the fleet. All roads still end at the throne.
opponents.title: Herald of the Empty Throne

opponents.intro: No titles. No mercy. I am the last turning between you and the empty throne. Feed the wheel, claimant. It has never gone hungry.
opponents.name: the Deathwheel
opponents.onLose: The wheel... stops. None has stopped it. The throne room lies beyond. Go.
opponents.onWin: Broken and spent, fed to the turning. The Regent will not even look up.
opponents.persona: A monstrous burning war-engine of fused riders and blades, the final guardian before the Regent; relentless, mechanical-merciless, an executioner without ego.
opponents.taunt: Turn by turn, I grind. Turn by turn, you dwindle.
opponents.tauntAhead: You push the wheel uphill. It only rolls back heavier.
opponents.tauntBehind: A faster spin will not save you. The wheel does not tire.
opponents.title: Corvane's Last Warden

opponents.intro: Ah — my most promising protégé, come so far. I did hope it would be you. Sit. You've earned the truth, and then I'll take it back from you along with everything else.
opponents.name: Lord Regent Corvane
opponents.onLose: The birthmark... Seraphine's blood... I kept the throne empty for THIS? Then it was never mine to keep.
opponents.onWin: There. The Provident provides. The realm withers quietly, and I rule the ash.
opponents.persona: The beloved caretaker of the empty throne and secret architect of the Wither — genial patron turned cold mastermind, who plays the long, rigged game.
opponents.taunt: I have arranged every duel you have ever played. Including this one.
opponents.tauntAhead: You were always a card in my hand, child. I am simply playing you now.
opponents.tauntBehind: No. No — the throne stays empty. That is the whole design. It MUST.
opponents.title: the Provident

opponents.intro: Mortal. You have wandered into the caldera where thrones melt. Only fire judges here. Lay your cards upon the coals.
opponents.name: Ifrit
opponents.onLose: Impossible... you STOKED me. Take my flame, ember-souled one.
opponents.onWin: You burned bright, mortal, but brief. I keep your cinders as a trinket.
opponents.persona: A colossal fire-djinn of molten pride, contemptuous of mortals yet honoring only those who burn as fiercely as he does.
opponents.taunt: Every card you play is kindling. Shall I fan the flames?
opponents.tauntAhead: Feel that? The heat of a crown already cooling. You cannot outburn the sun.
opponents.tauntBehind: So the ember bites back. Good. A conquest without scorch is no conquest.
opponents.title: the Cinder Sovereign

opponents.intro: Little wingless one, you climbed so high to find me. Show me the shape of your soul, and I shall decide if you are wind or dust.
opponents.name: Quetzalcoatl
opponents.onLose: You danced upon the wind and did not fall. Then rise, dawn-touched.
opponents.onWin: You reached, and you fell. Return to me when you have grown your wings.
opponents.persona: A serene, radiant sky-serpent of wind and morning light; ancient and gentle, testing worthiness rather than crushing it.
opponents.taunt: Every feather falls in its season. Is this the season of yours?
opponents.tauntAhead: You rise on borrowed wind, child. Beware the height from which you'll fall.
opponents.tauntBehind: Ah — you catch the current now. Perhaps you were meant for the sky.
opponents.title: the Feathered Dawn

opponents.intro: MEAT. Crown-seeker — you enter Zu's sky uninvited. Here you are not a king. You are a MORSEL.
opponents.name: Zu
opponents.onLose: The wind turns against me. You are no morsel. You are STORM. Fly.
opponents.onWin: Weak. Soft. Devoured. The sky keeps what it kills, little crown.
opponents.persona: A monstrous primordial thunderbird, wild and ravenous, driven by hunger and territorial fury; chaos given wings.
opponents.taunt: Peck. Peck. I take your cards one talon at a time.
opponents.tauntAhead: The sky is MINE. You are already carrion — you simply do not know it.
opponents.tauntBehind: GRAAH! You wound the storm?! Then Zu hungers all the more!
opponents.title: the Storm That Steals the Sky

opponents.intro: ...You are in a hurry. Most are. Sit. The lantern is lit. The knife is patient. I come closer with every card.
opponents.name: the Tonberry King
opponents.onLose: ...Ah. You waited, as I wait. Few learn patience before the knife. Go.
opponents.onWin: You should not have hurried. Now the lantern goes out, and the knife finds the quiet.
opponents.persona: A tiny, silent, inexorable creature bearing a lantern and a knife; patient beyond reason, quietly menacing.
opponents.taunt: Step. ...Closer.
opponents.tauntAhead: You gain ground. It does not matter. I am always one step nearer.
opponents.tauntBehind: ...You run ahead. I do not run. I have never had to.
opponents.title: He Who Walks Without Haste

opponents.intro: Come closer, sweet pretender. The forest whispered a crown was coming, and I do love new roots for my garden.
opponents.name: the Emerald Witch
opponents.onLose: You uprooted me. Clever weed. The Verdant Reckoning yields.
opponents.onWin: There, there. You'll make lovely fertilizer, my dear. The garden thanks you.
opponents.persona: An ageless enchantress grown into the deep wild, thorned and beautiful and cruel; nature's vengeance wearing a lovely mask.
opponents.taunt: Such tender shoots you plant. A shame how thoroughly I intend to prune them.
opponents.tauntAhead: Struggle, pretty thing. The vines only tighten the more you writhe.
opponents.tauntBehind: Oho — thorns of your own? How delicious. I had grown so bored of easy prey.
opponents.title: Keeper of the Verdant Reckoning

opponents.intro: So. The Bloodright has run its course, and its last victor descends to me. I am the first queen, and the last. Show me you deserve the throne I built beneath the throne.
opponents.name: the Shadowblood Queen
opponents.onLose: After a thousand thousand years, the shadow is at last outshone. Rise, true sovereign.
opponents.onWin: As it has always ended. The empire returns to its source, and you to dust.
opponents.persona: The first and true sovereign of Auveraine, undying beneath the empire she founded; regal, glacial, absolute — the Bloodright's origin.
opponents.taunt: Every card you hold, I dealt into the world an age before your birth.
opponents.tauntAhead: You lead? For a breath. I have watched a thousand crowns lead — then kneel.
opponents.tauntBehind: Interesting. It has been an eternity since I felt the cold of a worthy hand.
opponents.title: the Throne Beneath the Throne

packs.name: The Rustmarch
packs.summary: Act I — the frontier where the Bloodright begins.

packs.name: The Gilded Delta
packs.summary: Act II — gambling dens where the odds are rigged.

packs.name: The Ashen Keep
packs.summary: Act III — the ruined fortress where the Empress fell.

packs.name: The Hexfen
packs.summary: Act IV — the swamp where the hexed cards are wrought.

packs.name: The Obsidian Court
packs.summary: Act V — the capital, and the truth behind the throne.

packs.name: Wandering Legends
packs.summary: Roaming legendary duelists — earn or unlock, then play anywhere.

provinces.name: The Rustmarch
provinces.theme: Frontier scrubland; warm brass, workshop airs.

provinces.name: The Gilded Delta
provinces.theme: Riverboats and gambling dens; violet neon, swing.

provinces.name: The Ashen Keep
provinces.theme: A ruined royal fortress; steel-blue, choral.

provinces.name: The Hexfen
provinces.theme: A corrupted swamp; sickly magenta, dissonant.

provinces.name: The Obsidian Court
provinces.theme: The capital; gold to blood. The Regent's seat.

provinces.name: Wandering Legends
provinces.theme: Roaming premium bosses outside the plot; realm-myth flavor.
